section_path: power supply warnings and cautions
text: power supply warnings and cautions to reduce the risk of electric shock or damage to the equipment: do not disable the power cord grounding plug. the grounding plug is an important safety feature. plug the power cord into a grounded (earthed) electrical outlet that is easily accessible at all times. unplug the power cord from the power supply to disconnect power to the equipment. do not route the power cord where it can be walked on or pinched by items placed against it. pay particular attention to the plug, electrical outlet, and the point where the cord extends from the server. to reduce the risk of injury from electric shock hazards, do not open power supplies. refer all maintenance, upgrades, and servicing to qualified personnel. mixing different types of power supplies in the same server might: limit or disable some power supply features including support for power redundancy. cause the system to become unstable and might shut down. to ensure access to all available features, all power supplies in the same server should have the same output and efficiency ratings.

section_path: removing and replacing a boot device drive
text: raid 1 configuration, verify that the boot device ssds have the same model number and firmware version: in uefi system utilities, see system configuration > hpe ns204i boot controller > physical device information . configurations with ssds from different manufacturers are not supported. remove the failed drive and replace it with a new drive: slide the carrier out from the boot device cage. tilt the ssd at a 45° angle, and then remove the failed ssd from the m.2 slot. carefully press the ssd down to the horizontal position. if closed, pivot the carrier latch to open. slide the carrier with the new ssd into the boot device cage. make sure that the carrier latch is locked on the boot device cage. the boot device automatically rebuilds the raid 1 volume. if the newly installed ssd has a different model number as the ssd on the other drive carrier, replace the other ssd with one that is of the same model number as the new ssd. once both ssds are installed, the boot device automatically rebuilds the raid 1 volume.

text: re-entering the server serial number and product id after replacing the system board, re-enter the system serial number and product id. access the uefi system utilities. during post, press f9 . from the system utilities screen, select system configuration > bios/platform configuration (rbsu) > advanced options > advanced service options . select serial number , and then press enter . the serial number is modified by qualified service personnel and must match the serial number located on the chassis. product id is modified only by qualified service personnel. this value must match the product id located on the chassis. to confirm and save the settings, press f12 .

text: the cable connector. to prevent mechanical damage or depositing oil that is present on your hands, and other contamination, do not touch the ends of the connectors. before connecting a cable to a port, lay the cable in place to verify the length of the cable. use the internal cable management features to properly route and secure the cables. route cables so that they do not contact or rest on cooling components, including heatsinks. ensure that cable routing does not obstruct airflow to or from heatsinks or ventilation openings when routing cables, be sure that the cables are not in a position where they can be pinched or crimped. avoid tight bend radii to prevent damaging the internal wires of a power cord or a server cable. never bend power cords and server cables tight enough to cause a crease in the sheathing. make sure that the excess length of cables is properly secured to avoid excess bends, interference issues, and airflow restriction. before installing a new component or closing up the server, make sure that all cables are in

text: liquid cooling connector on the processor mezzanine board | option pn | color | from | to | |:------------|:--------|:-----------------------------|:--------------------------| | p78017-b21 | orange | direct liquid coolingm odule | liquid cooling conne ctor | connect the power cords using only the following supported configurations. using an unsupported power supply or cabling configuration can result in an unexpected loss of system power. the power supply cabling methods vary based on the selected power distribution unit (pdu). to ensure proper operation and the functionality of the circuit breaker, the input current rating of the power supply must be lower than the current rating of each breaker (node) in the pdu. the illustrations in this section are for logical mapping reference only. the actual power supply cabling will depend on the specific rack-pdu setup. for information about pdu compatibility with the server power supply, see the pdu quickspecs. pdu cabling: two-power supply configuration pdu cabling: four-power supply configuration

source_file: HPE_sd00006216en_us_HPE ProLiant Compute DL580 Gen12 Maintenance and Service Guide.pdf
section_path: pdu cabling: two-power supply configuration
text: pdu cabling: two-power supply configuration a two-power supply configuration supports the system domain. depending on the number of racks, couplets and storage, and the input voltage, connect this configuration to one or two pdus. if one of the pdus fails, the server continues to operate as long as the capacity of the remaining pdu feed is able to compensate for the entire domain load.

section_path: remove all power:
text: remove all power: disconnect each power cord from the power source. disconnect each power cord from the server. disconnect all peripheral cables from the server. remove the server from the rack. place the server on a flat, level work surface. look for any potential contact between the coolant and any of the internal cables and components, especially power connectors. cover the spill point with a dry absorbent material. remove the leaky liquid cooling module. avoid pushing out more coolant during removal. remove the system board. if the leak has made it to the system board or the chassis, do the following: lightly dampen a cleanroom wipe with deionized water. wring out any excess water, and gently wipe over areas with coolant residue. ensure that there is no more visible colored coolant or liquid residue. dry the system board in a 70°c environment for at least 8 hours. confirm that the system board is completely dry before reinstallation. follow the procedure in the server maintenance and service guide to reinstall the system board. repeat steps 11-18 on other servers affected by

section_path: water and electricity combined pose a significant safety hazard.
text: water and electricity combined pose a significant safety hazard. hardware electrical components that have been in contact with the spilled coolant might be damaged. to ensure a functional and safe server operation, identify and replace all damaged components. after the spill is properly cleaned up, do not rush to power on the system immediately. instead, leave the system in standby mode first and observe the front panel leds after connecting the power cables. verify if the system power led is illuminated. if not, replace the system board. in the ilo web interface or restful api, clear the coolant leakage status. for the detailed procedure, see the ilo 7 user guide ( https:/ /www.hpe.com/support/hpeilodocsquicklinks ). if the server uses a dlc cold plate module, reconnect the dlc hoses to the rack manifolds. power on the server. if the system fails to boot, replace the system board.
